_note: shadow_return = current_return = last_percent_change*100 (alpha_bot_execution.py:768,916). last_percent_change is Composer's per-day TODAY'S CHANGE (same field get_symphony_today_change uses), NOT a cumulative-since-open return. Chain-linking per-day returns via prod(1+r_i/100)-1 is CORRECT and intentional design per AC-M1F.3.2/PA-M1F-16. A position at -3% on each of 3 days has genuinely had -3% per-day and (0.97^3-1)*100 = -8.7327% cumulative is the correct answer. VERDICT: NOT A BUG.
description: Three consecutive trading days each recording shadow_return=-3.0%. Each -3% is today's daily return (last_percent_change*100). Chain-link CR = (0.97^3 - 1)*100 = -8.7327%. MDD of cumulative series [-3.0, -5.91, -8.7327] with peak=cum[0]=-3.0 is 5.7327%.
_tolerance_comment: rel=1e-9 appropriate — these are exact floating-point arithmetic on small fixtured values; tolerance guards only IEEE-754 rounding
